message:
feat(core): extract RegionQuiescenceSnapshot into a dedicated quiescence module

Lift the region-quiescence proof surface out of RegionTree's private
bookkeeping and into a small, deterministic module whose job is to
answer the question "why is this region not yet quiescent?" without
asupersync lab-oracle tests having to reach into RegionTree
internals.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/quiescence.rs (new, ~89 lines)

- ChildRegionQuiescence { region, state: Option<RegionState> } —
  per-child snapshot of a non-closed child that's still blocking
  its parent from becoming quiescent.
- RegionQuiescenceSnapshot { region, state, active_tasks,
  active_obligations, non_closed_children: Vec<ChildRegionQuiescence> }
  — the full deterministic view captured at a single observation
  point.  Derives PartialEq/Eq so tests can pin the exact snapshot.
- is_quiescent() method on the snapshot returns true iff active
  tasks == 0, active obligations == 0, and every child is Closed.
- Module-level docs explain the contract: snapshots are for
  diagnostics and lab-oracle tests; they do not drive scheduling.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/lib.rs
- Register pub mod quiescence; alphabetical insert between
  permeation_map and por to keep the module list tidy.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/region.rs
- Import ChildRegionQuiescence / RegionQuiescenceSnapshot.
- Split is_quiescent() into two functions: the existing
  fast-path boolean method now delegates to
  quiescence_snapshot(id).is_some_and(|s| s.is_quiescent()), and
  a new pub fn quiescence_snapshot(&self, id: Region) ->
  Option<RegionQuiescenceSnapshot> materializes the full diagnostic
  view.  Non-closed children are collected via filter_map so
  RegionState::Closed children are elided.
- complete_close() now captures the snapshot before failing, so
  the Internal error path has concrete context about which child
  regions or task/obligation counts are still open.  The diagnostic
  text is intentionally unchanged for now to keep test goldens
  stable; follow-up can embed the snapshot in the error once the
  RegionCloseError variant lands.

No behavior change for callers of is_quiescent(); this is a
source-compat refactor that exposes structured diagnostics without
touching the close-sequence state machine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
